The usual claim that Chinese medicine focused on the entire being applies to doctrinal statements better than to practice. Chinese physicians focused on the pathological processes that they thought created madness, and tried to modify these processes by means of drugs. Few of these doctors worked on selves. This folding of meaning on organic processes was facilitated by the general supervenience between mental and the physiological that most Chinese doctors took for granted and that was expressed in the very language they used (see 3.2).

But what physicians usually viewed as delirium, officials often treated as seditious speech. Instead of dismissing words as mad because they had been uttered by insane men, the emperor and his officials reacted as though these words competed with imperial claims, and as though the masses would be roused by them. The medical leveling of mad speech thus contrasted with the political exacerbation of its meaning.

Homicide trials in which madness was a possible factor created a new context in which legal and medical approaches could interact by discussing the same persons, the same

## PDF第637页

acts, and even the same pulses on the wrists of killers (see 12.4). But the power of these two approaches to carry consequences remained uneven. In judicial contexts, until the end of the Qing, the legal discourse had absolute primacy over medical categories and concerns. Since the medical belief that madness was curable and organically based was of little relevance to judicial practice, the optimism that most physicians displayed in their case records did not inform legal practice. And since the law accepted no gradation from the mad to the non-mad—madness was either absolute or irrelevant—no medico-legal expertise was allowed to roam this potentially controversial borderline.

I also studied the culturally ingrained nature of fantasies and the importance of the control of symbols to the cultural stability of the Qing government. Interactions between imperial officials and the fantasies of men considered insane would deserve more attention than I granted them. We know that the Qing state gave great importance to omens and symbols, but why be afraid of ostensibly mad men like Lin Shiyuan (see p. 1) when they spoke of “dragon mothers” and dream revelations? It appears that the trials I discussed in Chapters 14 and 15 attempted to suppress challenges to the very symbolic fabric of the state. These issues call for further investigation on the religious nature of Qing imperial rule, a field that has been pioneered by studies like Zito 1997, Wooldridge 2007, and Snyder-Reinke 2009, to name but a few.

We would also need to learn more about what people did when they did not understand mad behavior as a medical illness. We can safely surmise that the common people who treated madness with drugs were not in the mainstream. One reason why I did not discuss religious options at any length is that we seem to have few documents on them beyond prescriptive ritual compendia. Paul Katz (2009: 105-7) relied on twentieth-century documents and other people's fieldwork to describe "trials of the insane." But we could

## PDF第638页

probably find more information in fiction—Katz did mention some eighteenth-century
stories—on how families treated their mad relatives, in the same way as Edward Davis (2001)
used a collection of stories to reconstruct the nature of Song local religion.

Fiction could be put to many more uses. One feature of mad people in medical texts and popular perception was the peculiarity of their words. How did fictional sources portray mad speech? My impression is that mad characters in Chinese stories were usually misrecognized as mad before being revealed to be something else—playful immortals in disguise, hermits or visionaries, or people in the grip of real demons. Madness thus served to dissimulate something else, sometimes from the reader, but most often from other characters in the story. $ ^{6} $

Another issue I barely addressed is how educated people dealt with their own mental torments. What did men who resorted to neither medicine nor exorcism do when they felt themselves becoming crazy or saw their peers lose sanity? We could probably find several cases like that of Xu Nianzu 許念祖 (1655-1698), a scholar who committed suicide after repeated bouts of despair that his friends and correspondents referred to as madness (for references, see 8.5, p. 344n50).

This inventory of ignorance could linger. It reminds us that we are only beginning to understand how late imperial Chinese of various upbringing and types of expertise construed and handled people, acts, and words that they called “mad.”

## PDF第639页

BIBLIOGRAPHIES

## PDF第640页

# Bibliography A

Primary sources

In this bibliography, books are classified by the way I refer to them in the main text. This means that medical books are arranged by author names (with proper cross-references when I make exceptions) and legal books by titles. Because each bibliographical entry contains a lot of information, I put the book's author, its title, and its date of completion and first printing in bold type.

I refer to authors by their surname followed by their personal name (ming 名), which they received during childhood. I indicate their  $ \underset{\cdot}{z}\underset{\cdot}{i} $字 ("courtesy name," by which they were addressed socially) and bao 號 ("style name," which often contained a toponym) in parentheses.

Modern punctuated editions are re-editions, not “reprints.” When I say that a modern edition is “based on” a certain edition, I refer to the main edition (diben 底本) on which the recent edition is based. I do not list the other editions (jiaoben 校本) that the compilers also used for collation. Whenever possible, I try to cite from original editions or from photo-reproductions of originals. Throughout, I use “ed.” as short for “edition.”

Numbers like “#10934” after a book title refer to entry numbers in Quanguo Zhongyi tushu lianbe mulu 全國中醫圖書聯合目錄 [“National union catalogue of Chinese medical books and illustrations”], edited by the Library of the China Academy of Traditional Chinese Medicine (中國中醫研究院圖書館) under the direction of Xue Qinglu 薛清录 (Beijing: Zhongyi guji chubanshe, 1991). This catalogue lists the medical works that have survived in more than 100 Chinese libraries. Medical books that lack such a number are not listed in that catalogue, often because they have not survived in China.

When I enumerate the surviving editions of a given book, I refer to the surviving Chinese imprints in Chinese libraries. I count neither Japanese and Korean editions nor manuscript copies. The latest editions I cite are from 1911, the last year of the Qing dynasty.

Books noted in Chinese catalogues as “not divided into fascicles” (不分卷) are recorded here as being “in 1 juan.”

The archival collections I consulted for this research can be found below under the abbreviations LFZZ, XBDA, XKTB, and ZPZZ. UCDA also refers to archival documents.

2XMYLA. Erxu Mingyi lei'an 二續名醫類案 [“Second sequel to Mingyi lei'an”]. Editors in chief Lu Zhaolin 魯兆麟 et al. Shenyang: Liaoning kexue jishu chubanshe, 1996. 2 vols. A topically arranged selection of medical case files that were not included in or date from later than the Mingyi lei'an (see under Jiang Guan 江瓘) and Xu Mingyi lei'an (see under Wei Zhixiu).

Furen daquan liangfang 婦人大全良方 (#06607), in 24 juan; author's preface from 1247, with some later content. $ ^{1} $ Earliest known edition from the Yuan, re-edited in ChenZYQ. It was supplemented and re-edited in 1440 by famous book merchant Xiong Jun 熊鈞 (copy at Chongqing Library), and again in 1509 on the basis of that edition (copy at the National Library). Xue Ji's 薛己 heavily annotated recension (called Jiaozhu furen liangfang 校注婦人良方, preface 1547; see under Xue Ji) is now the best-known edition of Chen's work, but it has been criticized for modifying the original too much. It was nonetheless re-carved and reprinted a number of times in the centuries following its publication.

ChenZYQ. Chen Ziming yixue quanshu 陳自明醫學全書. Editor in chief Sheng Weizhong 盛維忠. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng 明清名醫全書大成. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Cheng'an xinbian 成案新編, in 15 juan, 1763?. Compiled by Min Wobei 閔我備.

Cheng'an zhiyi 成案質疑, in 31 juan, 1746. Edited by Hong Hongxu 洪弘緒 and Rao Jun 饒瀚.

## PDF第645页

Cheng Chong. See Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi xinfa 丹溪心法.

Cheng Congzhou 程從周 (zi Maoxian 茂先; from Yangzhou). Cheng Maoxian yian 程茂先醫案 (#10040), in 2 juan, ca. 1644. Original available at CATCM.

Cheng Guopeng 程國彭 (zì Zhongling 種齡; bao Pumingzi 普明子). Yixue xinwu 醫學心悟 (#05019), in 6 juan; 1732. MQXC ed. based on the 1732 original, which is available at CATCM.

Cheng Jie 程玠 (zì Songyai 松崖). Songyai yijing 松崖醫徑 (#04900), in 2 juan; preface from 1625. In ZYJ-2: 3-64.

Cheng Lun 程崙 (zì Yuanzhong 原種). Cheng Yuanzhong yian 程原種醫案 (#10030), in 6 juan; edition from 1625 available at CATCM.

Cheng Wuji 成無己 (1063/4-1156/7). Shanghai minglilun 傷寒明理論 [“Elucidating the principles of Cold injury”] (#00817), in 3 juan plus 1 juan of “Fanglun” 方論; 1142. Cheng YQ ed., based on 1601 edition by Wu Mianxue 無勉學. Song ed. at Guotu.

——. Zhujie shanghan lun 注解傷寒論 [Treatise on Cold Injury, Annotated and Explained] (#00591), in 10 juan. Printed in 1144.

ChengYQ. Cheng Wuji yixue quanshu 成無己醫學全書. Editor in chief 张国骏. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2004.

Chu Yushi 初虞世 (fl. late 11 $ ^{th} $ century; zì Hefu 和甫). Gujin luyan yangsheng biyong fang 古今錄驗養生必用方 [Proven Recipes from the Past and the Present Necessary for Cultivating Life], a.k.a. Yangsheng biyong fang 養生必用方, Chu Yushi fang 初虞世方, Biyong fang 必用方, and Yangsheng biyong 養生必用. Written in the Yuanfeng reign (1078-85), printed (for the 1 $ ^{st} $ time?) in 1097 as Chong gaizheng 重改正 gujin luyan yangsheng biyong fang. Lost, but cited 14 times in the Zhenglei bencao, as well as by Wang Ji in Waike lili, essay 87 (WangYQ: 379b).

Confucius. See Analects.

Da Ming... See Dai Ming...

Da Qing... See Dai Qing...

Dai Liang 戴良 (1317-83; zì Shuneng 叔能, bao Jiulingshan ren 九靈山人; from Jiulingshan in Pujiang 浦江 county, Jinhua 金華, Zhejiang). "Biography." See next entry.

——“Danxi weng zhuan”丹溪翁傳[“Biography of the Old Man of Danxi”].

Appended to the end of juan 5 of the Huizhou edition of the Danxi xinfa (see under Zhu Zhenheng). I cite from Danxi yiji (2000): 328-33.

——.Jiulingshan fang ji 九靈山房集, in 30 juan. Sibu beiyao 四部備要 edition.

Dai Mingshi 戴名世 (1653-1713; zì Tianyou 田有, bao Nanshan 南山). Dai Mingshi ji 戴名世集. Beijing: Zhonghua shuju, 1986.

## PDF第646页

Dai Ming shilu 大明實錄 [Veritable Records of the Great Ming]. In 173 volumes. Manuscript copy dating from the seventeenth or eighteenth century.

Dai Qing huidian shili 大清會典事例 [Supplementary Regulations of the Collected Canons of the Great Qing], in 1220 juan. 1899. Photoreprographic edition from Beijing: Zhonghua shuju: Xinhua shudian Beijing faxingsuo faxing, 1991. 12 vols.

Dai Qing lü 大清律. See Qing Code.

Dai Qing lũ jijie fuli 大清律集解附例. See Qing Code.

Dai Qing lüli 大清律例. See Qing Code.

Dai Qing lüli anyu 大清律例按語 [Commentaries on the Code of the Great Qing], in 104 volumes in 60 juan (10 ce 冊). Compiled by Pan Deshe 潘德畬. 1851 edition from Guangdong. Unpaginated.

Dai Qing lüli tongkao jiaozhu 大清律例通考校注, in 40 juan; 1886. An annotated edition of Wu Tan's 吳壇 Dai Qing lüli tongkao, which was completed in the late Qianlong reign (1736-1796). Punctuated edition from Beijing: Zhongguo zhengfa daxue chubanshe, 1992.

Dai Qing shilu 大清實錄 [Veritable Records of the Great Qing]. Dai Qing lichao shilu 大清歷朝實錄, in 4485 juan. Taipei: Hualian chubanshe, faxingzhe Huawen shudian, 1964. Divided into multi-volume sections named after individual emperors identified by their temple names.

Dai Qing xianxing xinglü 大清現行刑律例, in 45 juan; 1908. Compiled by Shen Jiaben 沈家本 (1840-1913) and others. In Xu xiu Siku quanshu 續修四庫全書, vol. 864.

Dai Qing xianxing xinglü anyu 大清現行刑律按語, in 39 juan, 1908. In Xu xiu Siku quanshu 續修四庫全書, vol. 864.

Dai Qing xinlüli 大清新律例, in 1 juan; 1909.

Dai Sigong 戴思恭 (1324-1405; zì Yuanli 原禮 [or 元禮], bao Suzhai 肅齋; from Pujiang county, Jinhua 金華, Zhejiang province). Bencao zhaichao 本草摘抄. Date unknown. Lost.

——.Jingui gouxuan. See under Zhu Zhenheng 朱震亨.

___. Leizheng yongyao 類證用藥 . Date unknown. Lost.

——. Tuiqiu shiyi 推求師意 (#10956), in 2.juan. Wang Ji's preface from 1534; another preface from that date announces a printing. Compiled by Wang Ji 汪機 and edited for printing by Wang's disciple Chen Jue 陳桷, who also chose the title (to point out that the book expounded on points that Zhu Zhenheng—Dai's master—had neglected in Jingui gouxuan). One edition from a 1633 collection called Wang Shishan yishu bazhong 汪石山醫書八種 [“Eight medical books by Wang (Ji) Shishan”] can be found at the CATCM library. No copy of the original edition is available in Beijing libraries.

Danxi xinfa. See under Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi xinfa 丹溪心法.

## PDF第647页

Danxi yiji 丹溪醫集. Second edition, collated by the Zhejiang sheng Zhongyiyao yanjiuyuan wenxian yanjiushi 浙江省中醫藥研究院文獻研究室. Beijing: Renmin weisheng chubanshe, 2001.

Danxi zuanyao. See Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi xiansheng yishu zuanyao 丹溪先生醫書纂要.

Daofa huiyuan 道法會元 [Taoist Methods, United by Principle]. A compendium compiled in the early fifteenth century. Taoist Canon (Daozang 道藏) edition.

Daoshan qinghua 道山清話, in 1 juan; early 12 $ ^{th} $ century. Compiler unknown. In Quan Song biji 全宋筆記, vol. 1. Zhengzhou, Henan: Daxiang chubanshe, 2006.

Ding Deyong 丁德用 (from Jiyang 濟陽 in Shandong). Nanjing buzhu 難經補注, in 5 juan; 1062.

Ding Ganren 丁甘仁 (1864-1924). Ding Ganren yian 丁甘仁醫案 (#10430), aka Dingshi yian 丁氏醫案, in 15 juan. First ed. from 1927, at CATCM.

Dingli huibian 定制匯編, in 23 juan. 1770 ed. by the Jiangxi provincial treasurer's office (江西布政司). Compiler unknown.

Dispelling Perplexities. See Li Gao, Neiwai shang bianbuo lun 内外傷辨惑論.

Divine Pivot. Huangdi neijing: Lingshu 黄帝内經靈樞 [Inner Canon of the Yellow Lord: Divine Pivot], in 18 juan and 81 pian. One recension of the Inner Canon.

DLCY. See Xue Yunsheng, Duli cunyi 讀例存疑.

Dong Ji 董汲 (from Dongping 東平 in Shandong). Banzhen beiji fang 斑疹備急方, in 1 juan; 1093 preface by Qian Yi 錢乙. Appended to Qian Yi's Xiaoer yaozheng zhijue 小兒藥證直訣 (q.v.).

Dong-ui bogam 東醫寶鑑 (Ch.: Dongyi baojian) (#11516), in 23 juan. 1611; first printed in 1613. Compiled by Heo Jun 許浚 (Ch.: Xu Jun) (1546-1615). Edition in simplified characters established and punctuated by Guo Aichun 郭靄春 et al., Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1996.

Dongyi baojian. See Dong-ui bogam.

Dongyuan shishu 東垣十書 (#11576). 1529. Contained 10 main works plus two appended ones. Only three were by Li Gao: Piwei lun, Lanshi micang, and Neiwai shang bianbuo lun (see these entries under Li Gao).

Dream of Red Mansions, in 100 chapters. Honglou meng 紅樓夢, by Cao Xueqin 曹雪芹.

Du Sijing 杜思敬 (b. 1234). See Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃.

Eighty One Problems. See Canon of Problems.

Elementary Learning. See Liu Chun, Yijing xiaoxue 醫經小學.

Enlightened Physician Pointing to his Palm. See Huangfu Zhong, Mingyi zhibhang 明醫指掌.

## PDF第648页

“Epitaph.” See Song Lian, “Gu Danxi xiansheng Zhu gong shibiao ci” 故丹溪先生朱公石表辭.

Er Cheng cui yan 二程粹言, in 2 juan. Collated by Yang Shi 楊時 (jinshi 1076), compiled by Zhang Shi 張栻. Preface by Zhang Shi dated 1166. SKQS ed. (Zibu 子部, Rujia lei 儒家類).

Essentials of the Golden Casket. Jingui yaolue 金匱要略 (#01195), in 3 juan. By Zhang Ji 張機 (zì Zhongjing 中景, bao Changsha 長沙), 196/220. Full title: Jingui yaolue fanglun 金匱要略方論 [Essentials of the Golden Casket, with Recipes and Discussions] (#01195). The earliest surviving edition is the Xinbian jingui yaolue fanglun 新編金匱要略方論 [Newly Collated Jingui yaolue fanglun] (1340). The Essentials of the Golden Casket, which was compiled from Treatise on Cold Injury and Miscellaneous Illnesses (q.v.), concerned only miscellaneous illnesses.

Excellent Recipes. See Chen Ziming, Furen daquan liangfang 婦人大全良方.

Fang Bao 方苞 (1668-1749). Fang Bao ji 方苞集. Shanghai: Shanghai guji chubanshe, 1983. 2 volumes. Collated and punctuated by Liu Jigao 劉季高.

Fang Guang. See Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi xinfa fuyu 丹溪心法附餘.

Fangjue. See Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi michuan fangjue 丹溪秘傳方訣.

Fang Lue 万略 (zì Nanxun 南薰; from Wuning 武寧 in Jiangxi). Shangyou tang yian 尚友堂醫案 (#10132), in 2 juan; printed in 1846 by the Shangyou Hall 尚友堂. Copies of the original edition survive at the libraries of the Shanghai Zhongyi xueyuan and the Zhejiang Zhongyi xueyuan. Photo-reproduction at Shanghai guji shudian 上海古籍書店 (1979).

Fang Youzhi 方有執. Shanghai un tiaobian 傷寒論條辨 (#00593), in 8 juan. Author's preface 1589; first printed in 1591.

Fei Chengzu 費承祖 (1851-1914; zì Shengfu 繩甫). Fei Shengfu yihua yī'an 費繩甫醫話醫案 (#10384); 1913. Hand copies at Shanghai and Nanjing Zhongyi xueyuan.

Feng Zhaozhang 馮兆張 (zì Chuzhan 楚瞻; from Haiyan 海鹽 in Zhejiang). Fengshi jinnang milu zazheng daxiao hecan 馮氏錦囊祕錄雜證大小合參 (#11623), in 20 juan; author's preface 1694. First extant printed edition from 1722. Two other Kangxi eds. of unknown dates.

FengYQ. Feng Zhaozhang yixue quanshu 馮兆張醫學全書. Editor in chief Tian Sisheng 田思胜. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Florilegium on Saving Lives. See Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃.

Fontanelle Classic. See Luxin jing 顱顱經.

FYYC. See Li Wenrong, Fang Yuyicao 傲寓意草.

Gao Bin. See Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi xiansheng zbifa xinyao 丹溪先生治法心要.

Gao Suzhong. See Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi xiansheng zhifa xinyao 丹溪先生治法心要.

Ge Hong 葛洪 (ca. 284-364). Zhouhou beiji fang 肘候備急方 (#02928). Supplemented by Tao Hongjing 陶弘景 (456-536) and expanded by Yang Yongdao 楊用道 (Jin dynasty, 1115-1234). SKQS ed.

Golden Casket. See Essentials of the Golden Casket.

Gong Juzhong 龔居中 (𠀤𠀤) Yingyuan 應園, bao Ruxuzi 如虛子 and Shoushi zhuren 壽世主人). Tanhuo dianxue 痰火點雪 (#06393), a.k.a. Honglu dianxue 紅爐點雪, in 4 juan; author's preface from 1630. One Ming edition (by Liu Dayi 劉大易 from Jianyi shulin 建邑書林) at Anhui Provincial Library in Hefei. Other surviving editions: 1804 (2), 1813, Jiaqing, 1840, 1899. Also in ZYD-5: 27-89.

Gong Tingxian 龔廷賢 (ca. 1538-1635; zì Zicai 子才, Yunlin 雲林, bao Wuzhenzi 悟真子).

Gujin yijian 古今醫鑒 [The Mirror of Medicine Past and Present] (#04924), in 16 juan (1576). Extant copies of eds. from 1577, 1589, Wanli, 1684 (3), Kangxi, Qing. An earlier version of this work had been compiled by Gong Xin 龔信, Gong Tingxian's father.

Shoushi baoyuan 壽世保元 (#04939), in 10 juan; 1615. One extant copy of a Ming edition at Beijing Zhongyi xueyuan tushuguan. Extant editions: 1667, 1732, 1755, 1775, 1797, 1802, 1812, 1817 (3), Jiaqing, 1823 (3), 1829 (2), 1831, 1834, 1843, 1844, 1850, Daoguang, 1858 (2), 1862 (2), 1863, 1868, 1869 (2), 1880, 1886, 1888, 1900, 1903, 1904, 1905, 1906, 1907, 1908, Guangxu (3), 1909 (3), 1911, late Qing, other Qing (18). I cite from the GongYQ ed., which is based on the Ming ed.

——. Wanbing huichun 萬病回春 [Returning to Spring from the Myriad Illnesses] (#04926), in 8 juan (1587 preface where he announces he wrote Gujin yijian (see above) when he was around 40 [趺年]). Extant editions from 1588, 1602, Ming, 1662, Kangxi, 1742, 1754, 1767, 1778, 1800, 1816, 1828 (2), 1837, 1845, 1858, 1870, 1901, unknown, Qing (2). (also many seventeenth-century Japanese eds. and Republican eds.)

Yao xing ge 藥性歌 (#02656). Appended to Gong's Wanbing buichun and Shoushi baoyuan (qq.vn.), as well as to Shao Da's 1622 edition of the Mingyi zhi-zhang (see under Huangfu Zhong).

——. Yilin zhuangyuan jishi quanshu 醫林狀元濟世全書 (#04940), in 8 juan (1616).

In BDYS-2: photo-lithographic reproduction of a 1638 Japanese edition from the 村上平樂寺, itself a reprint of a Ming Wanli-era edition from Wanjuan Lou Cunyang 萬卷樓存義堂 (a partial Ming edition survives at CATCM).

——. Yunlin shengou 雲林神彀 [Yunlin's divine archer] (#04927), in 4 juan (1591). Extant copies of eds. from 1591, Wanli, late Ming, 1662, 1843 (3), Daoguang (2), 1867, 1875, 1876, 1886, Qing (5).

—. Zhongxing xianfang 種杏仙方 (#3037), in 4 juan; author's preface from 1581. An incomplete Ming ed. is the earliest extant one. Xue 1991 claims that a copy kept at the Gugong bowuyuan dates to 1581, but an expert suspects that it is an early Qing edition

(see GongYQ [1999], "Jiaozhu shuoming" 校注說明, p. 1). GongYQ ed., based on a 1650 Japanese ed.

GongYQ. Gong Tingxuan yixue quanshu 龔廷賢醫學全書. Editors in chief Li Shihua 李世华 and Wang Yuxue 王育学. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Gouxuan. See Zhu Zhenheng, Jingui gouxuan 金匱鉤玄.

Grand Basis. Huangdi neijing: Taisu 黄帝内经太素 [Inner Canon of the Yellow Lord: Grand Basis]. Compiled by Yang Shangshan 楊上善 on 656 CE or soon afterwards.

Great Learning. Daxue 大學. One chapter from the Liji 禮記, edited by Zhu Xi; one of the "Four Books."

Gu Jinshou 顧金壽 (zì Xiaolan 曉瀾). Wumen zhiyan lu 吳門治驗錄 (a.k.a. Gu Xiaolan xiansheng yian 顧曉瀾先生醫案) (#10116), in 1 juan; 1823.

Gujin tushu jicheng 古今圖書集成, in 10,000 juan. 1726. Edited by Chen Menglei 陳孟雷 and completed by Jiang Tingxi 蔣廷錫.

Gujin yitong zhengmai daquan. 古今醫統正脈大全 (#11588). 1601. A large compendium of medical books compiled in Huizhou under the supervision of Wang Kentang 王肯堂 and Wu Mianxue 無勉學.

Gu Yanwu 顧炎武 (1613-1682; zì Tínglin 亭林). Rizhi lu 日知錄, in 32 juan. SKQS ed.

Guangyun 廣韻. Compilation started in 1007 on imperial order and was completed in 1011. Called Da Song chongxin guangyun 大宋重修廣韻, it was based on an earlier book of the same name, to which characters had been added several times since its first publication in 601. The SKQS called the latter book Yuanben Guangyun 原本廣韻.

Guide to Clinical Practice. See Ye Gui, Linzheng zhinan yian 臨證指南醫案.

Guo Jizhong. See Chanyu baoqing ji 產育保慶集.

Guotu. Guojia tushuguan 国家图书馆, or National Library, in Beijing. Formerly the Beijing tushuguan 北京图书馆 (Beijing Library), or "Beitu."

Guo Yong 郭雍. Shanghai buwang lun 傷寒補亡論 (#00821), in 20 juan; 1181. Earliest edition from the Wanli era (1573-1620). Two Qing editions from 1821 and 1911.

Han Dihe 韓祗和. Shanghai weizhi lun 傷寒微旨論 [On the Subtle Essentials of Cold Injury] (#00811), in 2 juan; 1086. Earliest extant edition from the SKQS (1782).

Han Mao 韓懋. Hanshi yitong 韓士醫通 (#10636), in 2 juan; 1522. Original ed. at Junshi yixue kexueyuan (Beijing). 1532 ed. at Shoudu tushuguan (Beijing). Also in ZYD-5.

HDSL. See Dai Qing huidian sbili 大清會典事例.

Hejiju fang 和劑局方，in 10 juan. Edited by Chen Shiwen 陳師文，Pei Zongwen 裴宗文，and Chen Cheng 陳承；1107/10. Revised and supplemented edition of the Taiyiju fang 太醫局方 (1078/85)，retitled after the Shouyaosuo 售藥所 had been renamed Hejiju

## PDF第651页

和劑局. Supplemented in 1151 and renamed Taiping huimin hejiju fang 太平惠民和劑局方 (#02972) (because Hejiju had been renamed Huimin hejiju in 1148). Supplemented again in 1208 with an appendix called Zhinan zonglu 指南總錄, by Xu Hong; still more recipes added during the Baoyou and Chunyou reigns.

He Mengyao 何夢瑤 (1693-1764; zì Baozhi 報之, bao Xichi 西池). Yi bian 醫碥 (#05035), in 7 juan; 1751. First ed. at Guotu (incomplete first ed. at CATCM; complete later ed. at CATCM).

History of the Jin. Jinshi 金史. Compiled by Tuotuo 脫脫 [Toghto] (1313-1355); presented in 1344. Zhonghua shuju edition.

History of the Ming. Mingshi 明史. Compiled by Zhang Tingyu 張廷玉 (1672-1755); presented in 1739. Zhonghua shuju edition.

History of the Northern Dynasties. Beishi 北史. Compiled by Li Yanshou 李延壽 (fl. 618-676); presented in 659. Zhonghua shuju edition.

History of the Song. Songshi 宋史. Compiled by Tuotuo 脱脱 [Toghto] (1313-1355); presented in 1345. Zhonghua shuju edition.

History of the Southern Dynasties. Nanshi 南史. Li Yanshou 李延壽 (fl. 618-676); presented in 659. Zhonghua shuju edition.

History of the Yuan. Yuanshi 元史. Compiled by Song Lian 宋濂; presented in 1370. Zhonghua shuju edition.

Hong Zun 洪遵. Jiyan fang 集驗方 [Anthology of Tested Recipes]. Lost

Huashu 化書 [Book of Transformations]. Attributed to Wenchang 文昌.

Hua Xiuyun. See Ye Gui, Linzheng zhinan yian 臨證指南醫案.

Huai Yuan 懷遠 (zì Baoqi 抱奇). Gujin yiche 古今醫徹 [#05803], in 4 juan; two prefaces dated 1808; first printed in 1810. Surviving eds.: 1810 (at Zhongguo yixue kexueyuan and Shanghai Library), 1830 (at CATCM). In ZYJ-2: 65-133.

Huangdi bashiyi nanjing. See Canon of Problems.

Huangdi neijing 黄帝内經. Ca. 1 $ ^{st} $ century BCE. See Divine Pivot, Basic Questions, and Grand Basis.

Huangdi neijing: Lingshu. See Divine Pivot.

Huangdi neijing: Suwen. See Basic Questions.

Huangdi neijing: Taisu. See Grand Basis.

Huangfu Mi. See AB Canon of Acumoxa.

Huangfu Zhong 皇甫中 (bao Yunzhou 雲洲; from Renhe 仁和, i.e., Hangzhou). Mingyi zhizhang 明醫指掌 [#04916], in 10 juan; 1579. Supplemented by Wang Kentang 王肯堂, and again by Shao Da 邵達 (zi Xingfu 行甫). As the final editor, Shao wrote the fanli, where he mentioned the "old edition" 舊刻. Preface from 1622. A 1579 ed. (by

## PDF第652页

Shulin Liushi Anzheng Hall 書林劉氏安正堂) survives at Zhongguo yixue kexueyuan. One 1622 ed. at CATCM, 1622 (with works on 藥性) also at CATCM, Ming (2). Qing eds.: 1657, 1699, 1755, 1779, 1794, Qianlong, 1811, 1814, 1818, 1820, Jiaqing, 1843, 1858 (2), 1895, 1904, Guangxu, 1911, Qing. Xue 1991 dates the original book to 1556, perhaps from the preface of the 1579 ed., which I have not consulted. I cite from the MQXC ed., which is based on a "Ming edition" (probably the 1622 ed., one copy of which is found at CATCM).

Huang Yuanyu 黄元御 (1705-1758). Sisheng xinyuan 四聖心源 (#00302), in 10 juan; author's preface 1753. SKCM 54: 140-232. Photolithographic reproduction of an edition found in the Huangshi yishu bazhong (Chengdu: TZ7 [1868]; originally Changsha: Xiehejing she 燮和精舍, XF10 [1860]), held at the library of the Zhongguo kexue yuan. HuangYQ ed. is based on an 1842 edition.

(by other people). Huangshi yishu bazhong 黄氏遗書三種. 1868. Contains eight books by Huang Yuanyu.

HuangYQ. Huang Yuanyu yixue quanshu 黄元御醫學全書. Editor in chief Sun Qiaxi 孫洽熙. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Huilin 慧琳. Yiqiejing yinyi 一切經音義 [The sounds and meanings (of words) in the Canon of Everything]. Completed in 808.

HWHG. Haiwai buigui Zhongyi shanben guji congshu 海外回歸中醫善本古籍叢書. 12 vols. Beijing: Renmin weisheng chubanshe, 2003. Punctuated editions in simplified characters of Chinese medical books that are no longer extant in China.

Inner Canon. Huangdi neijing 黄帝内经. Ca. 1 $ ^{st} $ century BCE. See Divine Pivot, Basic Questions, and Grand Basis.

Jihong 濟洪. Danliao jiyan fang 澹寮集驗密方. Lost.

Jijiu pian 急就篇. Edited by Shi You 史游 under the reign of Han Yuandi 元帝 (48-33 BCE) and later supplemented by other people. Commentary by Yan Shigu 彦師古 (581-645). SKQS ed.

Jiyun 集韻, in 10 juan; 1039. Compiled on imperial orders by a team led by Ding Du 丁度. Original ed. kept at Guotu, photo-reproduced in Songke Jiyun 宋刻集韻 (Beijing: Zhonghua shuju, 1989; 2005 reprint).

Jiajian cheng'an xinbian 加減成案新編. Prefaces from 1834. Collated by Xu Lian 許槤 and Xiong E 熊莪. In Xuxiu Siku quanshu 續修四庫全書, vols. 865-66.

Jiaqing shilu 嘉慶實錄. Dai Qing Renzong Rui huangdi shilu 大清仁宗睿黃帝實錄.

“Veritable Records” of the Jiaqing emperor (r. 1796-1820). Volumes 47-53 of Dai Qing shilu (q.v.)

Jiang Guan 江瓘 (zī Minying 民瑩) and Jiang Shaowei 少微 (zī Yingsu 應宿), comp. Mingyi leian 名醫類案 (#10020), in 12 juan. Jiang Guan's preface from 1549; not printed when he died. His son re-edited it lightly and printed it in 1591 (the date of his

## PDF第653页

colophon); the fanli seems to have been by him. Copy of first ed. at CATCM. I cite from SKQS ed.

Jiao Hong 焦竑 (1541-1620). Guochao xianzheng lu 國朝獻徵錄, in 120 juan.

Jingui gouxuan. See under Zhu Zhenheng.

Jinghua yuan 鏡花緣, in 100 chapters. By Li Ruzhen 李汝珍 (ca. 1763 – ca. 1830).

Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃 (#11566), in 19 juan; 1308/15. Compiled by Du Sijing 杜思敬 (b. 1234). Complete Yuan ed. at Liaoning sheng Zhongyi xueyuan (Shenyang). Two incomplete Yuan editions at Guotu (one has 粹 in the title, the other 萃), but they form a quasi-complete edition between the two of them (edition A has juan 3 to 7, 9 to 13, and 17 to 19; ed. B has 1 to 6 and 11 to 19). Another incomplete ed. at Beijing University.

Jingui yaolue fanglun. See Essentials of the Golden Casket.

Jingyue quanshu fahui 景岳全書發揮 (#11528), in 1 juan. Earliest known edition from 1844. Falsely attributed to Ye Gui 業桂.

Jiu Tang shu 舊唐書. See Old Book of Tang.

Kangxi Dictionary. Kangxi zidian 康熙字典, in 36 juan; 1716.

Kangxi shilu 康熙實錄. Dai Qing Shengzu Ren huangdi shilu 大清聖祖仁皇帝實錄.

“Veritable Records” of the Kangxi emperor (r. 1662-1722). Volumes 8-13 of Dai Qing shilu (q.v.).

Kong Qi 孔奇. Zhizheng zhiji 至正值記 [Frank recollections of the Zhizheng (1341-67) reign], in 4 juan, ca. 1365. Beijing: Zhonghua shuju, 1991.

Kongzi. See Analects.

Kosoto Hiroshi 小曾戶洋, Shinohara Kōichi 篠原孝市, and Maruyama Toshiaki 丸山敏秋. 1981. Tōyō igaku zempon sōsho 東洋醫學善本叢書 [Collected rare books on Oriental medicine]. 8 vols. Osaka: Tōyō igaku kenkyūkai.

Kou Zongshi 寇宗奭. Bencao yanyi 本草衍義 [Elaborations on materia medica] (#02239), in 20 juan; 1116, first printed in 1119. May have been originally called Bencao guangyi 本草廣義. Author's preface explains that the book was designed to expound on the Bencao tujing 本草圖經 (1062) and the Jiayou bencao 嘉祐本草 (1061).

Lei Menglin 雷夢麟. Du lũ suo yan 讀律瑣言 [Trifling Words on the Code], in 30 juan plus one appended juan; Jiajing era [1522-1566]. 2000 ed. at Beijing: Falü chubanshe, established and punctuated by Huai Xiaofeng 懷效鋒 and Li Jun 李俊, based on a 1562 re-edition by She 歙 county magistrate Xiong Bingwen 熊秉元 and an incomplete 1557 ed. by Luzhou 盧州 prefect Wang Keyong 汪克用.

Leipian. See Sima Guang, Leipian 類篇.

## PDF第654页

LFZZ. Lufu zouzhe 錄副奏摺 [“Secretarial copies of palace memorials”]; also known as “Junjichu lufu zouzhe” 軍機處錄副奏摺 [“Secretarial copies of palace memorials of the Grand Council”], “Lufu” 錄副, or the “Archives of the Grand Council.” Held at the First Historical Archives in Beijing. I cite by microfilm numbers.

Li'an quanji 例案全集, in 35 juan; compiler's preface from 1722. First printed in 1731. Compiled by Zhang Guangyue 張光月.

Li'an xuzeng quanji 例案續增全集, in 41 juan; compiler's preface from 1759. Compiled by Shen Rutun 沈如焞.

Li Chan 李梴. Yixue rumen 醫學入門 (#11512), in 7 juan plus one opening juan; 1575 [though includes a text from 1580]. Original ed. at CATCM. "Ming-Qing mingzhu congkan" 明清名著叢刊 ed. (Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1996), based on the 1575 ed.

Li Gao 李杲 (zì Mingzhi 明之; bao Dongyuan 東垣; ca. 1181-1251). Dongyuan shixiao fang 東垣試效方 (#02976), in 9 juan. Edited by Luo Tianyi 盧天益 in the 13 $ ^{th} $ century.

——. Neiwai shang bianhuo lun 内外傷辨惑論 [Dispelling Perplexities on Inner and Outer Harm (#06183), in 3 juan; author's preface 1247. Earliest extant ed. 1487; most common ed.: Dongyuan shishu 東垣十書 (1529). Possibly edited into a book at some unknown time on the basis of dispersed texts, since unlike Li's other works, it did not appear in the early fourteenth-century Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃 (q.v.).

___. Piwei lun 脾胃論 [Treatise on the Spleen and Stomach] (#06184), in 3 juan; 1249 preface by Yuan Haowen 元好問 (1190-1257).

——.Shanghan huiyao 傷寒會要. After 1232. Lost.

——. Yixue faming 醫學發明 [Elucidations on medical learning] (#06185), in 1 juan.

——. See also Dongyuan shishu 東垣十書.

Li Guangdi 李光地 (1642-1718). Li Guangdi nianpu 李光地年普.

LiGYQ. Li Dongyuan yixue quanshu 李東垣醫學全書. Editor in chief Zhang Nianshun 張年順. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe. Presents the works of Li Gao 李杲.

Li Lian 李濂 (1488-1566; jǐnshì 1523; zì Chuanfu 川父; from 祥符 [Kaifeng] in Henan).

Yishi 醫史 [“History of Medicine”] (#11311), in 10 juan, 1513? Photoreproduction of a Zhengde imprint (perhaps first ed.) held at the Shanghai Library in SKCM 42: 221-306.

Li Shengchun 李盛春. Yixue yanyue 醫學研悅 (#11596), in 10 juan. Collectanea composed of 6 different books (including Zhang Heteng's 張鶴騰. Shangshu quanshu 傷署全書 [#05733], in 2 juan; author's preface from 1623. First printed in 1626 (one copy at Shandong Zhongyiyao daxue). MQXC ed. (1997) based on the 1626 ed.

Li Shisheng. See Chanyu baoqing ji.

## PDF第655页

Li Shizhen 李時珍 (1518-1593; zì Dongbi 東璧, bao Pinhu shanren 瀕湖山人; from Qizhou 蘄州 in Hubei). Bencao gangmu 本曹綱目 (#02261), in 50 juan; completed in 1578; first printing started in 1593 and finished in 1596. I cite from the SKQS ed.

Li Tingxia 李延昰. Majue huibian 脈訣匯辨 (#01416), in 10 juan; 1666. Original ed. at Zhongguo kexueyuan library.

Li Wenrong 李文榮 (zǐ Guanxian 冠仙, bie hao Rumei laoren 如眉老人; from Dantu 丹徒 county in Jiangsu). Fang Yuyi cao 仿寓意草 (#10124), in 2 juan. 34 cases. Preface from 1835, but contains later cases. 1887 ed. available at the Zhongguo yixue kexueyuan. Re-edited in Sansan yishu 三三醫書 (1923) based on that 1887 ed. I cite from SSYS-3 (1998 [1923]).

——. Li Guanxian yian 李冠仙醫案 (#10133), in 1 juan. 27 cases, a slightly abridged copy of Li's Fang Yuyi cao (see previous entry). In ZGZC-17, from manuscript held at the Shanghai Library.

——. Zhiyi bibian 知醫必辨 (#10734), in 1 juan; 1849. 1918 ed. by Shaoxing yiyaoxue baoshe 紹興醫藥學報社 at CATCM. MYYH ed. based on ZYD ed. (1936).

Li Yongcui 李用粹 (zǐ Xiuzhi 修之, bao Xing'an 惺庵; fl. early Qing; from Yunjian 云間 in Jiangsu). Jiude tang yian 舊德堂醫案 (#10047), in 1 juan; late 17th century. Printing announced in author's preface, but no surviving edition from the time. First extant edition from 1923 (in SSYS), based on a manuscript copy of an earlier edition, which appears to be lost. I cite from SSYS-1 (1998 [1923]): 457-74.

——. Zhengzhi huibu 證治匯補 (#06207), in 8 juan. First edition from 1687. I consulted a 1691 ed. at CATCM. Extant eds.: 1687, 1691, Kangxi (2), 1883, 1892, Qing (2). Also in SSYS.

Li Zhongli 李中立. Bencao yuanshi 本草原始 (#02271), in 12 juan; 1612. Edition from 1638 at CATCM.

Li Zhongzi 李中梓 (zī Shicai 士材, 1588-1655). Bencao tongxuan 本草通玄 [“Penetrating the mysteries of materia medica”] (#02288), in 2 juan; first published in Li Zhongzi's posthumous Shicai sanshu 士才醫書 (1667; see below). Surviving editions: 1678 (by Wu Sangui 吳三桂 in Yunnan!), Qing (3).

——. Bingji shazhuan 病機沙篆 [Sand seal on the mechanisms of illness] (#06204), in 2 juan. First published in 1667 in Li Zhongzi's posthumous Shicai sanshu (q.v.). Other surviving editions: 1676, 1910, Qing (2).

——. Lizhong yian 里中醫案 [Medical case files from the village]. Also known as Li Zhongzi yi'an 李中梓醫案 (#10043); first published in 1666 in Li Tingxia's Maijue buibian 脈訣匯辨 (q.n.). I cite from the LiZhYQ edition, which is based on a Qing handwritten copy, perhaps the original of a manuscript edition collated by Li Zhongzi's fourth-generation descendant (1676 postscript by compiler).

——. Neijing zhiyao 内經知要 [Knowing the essentials of the Inner Canon] (#00039) in 2 juan; 1642.

## PDF第656页

——. Shanbu yisheng weilun 刪補頤生微論 [“Subtle discussions on nourishing life, emended and supplemented”] (#11520), in 4 juan; 1642. Originally Yisheng weilun (see below), 1618. In SKCM-46 (based on 1642 ed.).

——. Shanghai kuoyao 傷寒括要 [The essentials of Cold Injury] (#00867), in 3 juan (plus 2 juan of recipes); 1649. Based on Li's Shanghai shouzhu 傷寒授珠 [Teaching the pearls of Cold Injury medicine] (1645), which was lost during the Qing conquest of Jiangnan.

Shicai sanshu 士材三書 [Three books by Shicai] (#11610), in 9 juan, 1667. Compiled by Li's disciple You Cheng 尤乘. Contains three of Li's books (Zhenjia zhengyan, Bencao tongxuan, and Bingji shazhuan [see individual entries]), plus a book titled Shoushi qingbian 壽世青編 (2 juan) by compiler You Cheng. Original ed. at CATCM. Surviving eds.: 1667, 1708, Kangxi, 1728, 1767, 1769, 1783, 1804, 1818, 1887, 1905, 1907, Guangxu, 1909, Qing (12).

——. Yaoxing jie 藥性解 [Explanations on the nature of medical drugs], in 1 juan, 1619. Printed in 1622 with additions by Qian Yunzhi 錢允治 on how to prepare drugs for medicinal use.

Yisheng weilun 頤生微論 [Subtle discussions on nourishing life] (#11520, entry 1); 1618 (ed. by Ye Yangfeng's Shulin [Jianyang] 書林葉仰峰 at Beida). See Shanbu yisheng weilun above.

Yizong bidu 醫示必讀 [Required readings on the pedigree of medicine] (#11519), in 10 juan; 1637. Extant eds.: 1637, Ming (2), 1649, 1686, 1687, 1710, Kangxi, 1749, 1756, 1759, 1770, 1774, 1775, 1779, 1782, 1790, 1793, 1801, 1815, Jiaqing, 1822, 1836, 1860, 1864, 1883 (2), 1886, 1888, 1889, 1895, 1898, 1900, 1902, 1904, 1905, 1907 (2), Guangxu, Qing (12).

——. Zhenjia zhengyan 診家正眼 [The correct eye of the diagnostician] (#01506), in 3 juan; first printed in 1660 (print at CATCM) and again in 1667 in two editions: in Li Zhongzi's Shicai sanshu (see above) and in a joint edition with You Cheng's Shoushi qingbian 壽世青編 (q.v.). Four other independent but undated Qing editions survive.

LiZhYQ. Li Zhongzi yixue quanshu 李中梓醫學全書. Editor in chief Bao Laifa 包來发. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Liang Xuemeng 梁學孟 (zì Renfu 仁甫). Guoyi zongzhi 國醫宗旨 [The Pedigree and Purport of the Country's Medicine] (#06194), in 4 juan; printed in 1607. One copy of an unidentified edition survives at Shanghai Zhongyi xueyuan Library.

——. Tanhuo zhuanmen 痰火颛门 (#06392), in 4 juan; printed in 1610. One copy of an edition by Chen Suoxue 陳所學, from Jianyang 建陽 (Fujian), survives at Suzhou City Library.

Lin Peiqin 林佩琴 (1772-1839; zǐ Yunhe 雲和; from Danyang 丹陽 in Jiangsu). Leizheng zhicai 類證治裁 (#05130), in 8 juan; author's preface 1839, first printed in 1851. Surviving eds.: 1851, 1860, 1868, 1874, 1884, 1892, 1893, 1897, Guangxu. MQXC ed. based on an 1884 ed.

## PDF第657页

Lingshu. See Divine Pivot.

Liu Chun 劉純 (1340-1412; zì Zonghou 宗厚 [sometimes Jinghou 景厚]). Shanghai zhili 傷寒治例 (#00833), in 1 juan. Edited and printed in 1479 by Xiao Qian 蕭謙 (jinshi 1475). See Appendix 4.

Yijing xiaoxue 醫經小學 [Elementary Learning on the Medical Canons] (#04891), in 5 juan; completed in 1388. First edition at Junshi yixue kexue yuan. 1473 ed. by Xiong Zongli's Zhongde Hall at Shanghai Library. See Appendix 4.

——. Zabing zhili 雜病治例, in 1 juan, 1408? (not in Xue 1991). Compiled (編輯) by Liu Chun and edited (校正) and printed in 1479 by Xiao Qian 蕭謙 (jinshi 1475), who claims he obtained the book from Liu's descendants. Original 1479 ed. at Liaoning Zhongyi xueyuan, photo-reproduced in SKCM 41: 435-66. See Appendix 4.

—— and Xu Yanchun 徐彥純 (zì Yongcheng 用誠; d. 1384). Yuji weiyi 玉機微義 [Subtle Meanings of the Secrets Carved in Jade] (#04892), in 50 juan. Completed in 1396. Printed in Xi'an in 1439 and 1440, and many more times during the Ming dynasty. See Appendix 4.

LiuChYQ. Liu Chun yixue quanshu 劉純醫學全書. Editor in chief Jiang Dianhua 姜典华. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Liu Qi 劉祁. Guiqian zhi 歸潛志 [Record of (the studio of) 'Returning (Home) to Obscurity']，in 14 juan. Author's preface 1259. Beijing: Zhonghua shuju, 1983.

Liu Quande 劉全德 (zi Yiren 一仁). Kaozheng bingyuan 考證病源 (#04929), in 3 juan. 1599. A manuscript copy is held at the Shanghai Library, but the same book can be found in juan 5 to 7 of Zhu Shijin's Yijian nengyi 一見能醫 (q.v.) under the name Bingyin fu 病因賦, of which Liu Quande is only presented as the annotator. In ZGZC-2.

Liu Wansu 劉完素 (1120?–1200; zì Shouzhen 守真; bao Hejian 河間). Baoming jí 保命集. See Liu's Sunven bingji qiyi baoming ji.

——. Huangdi Suwen xuanming lun fang 黄帝素問宣明論方 (#00186), in 15 juan. Before 1186 and before Liu's Yuanbing shi (see below); perhaps 1179. $ ^{2} $ Usually referred to as Xuanming lun. Mentioned in Ma Zongsu's 馬宗素 preface to Tujie Sunen yaozhi lun (see next entry), which also carries a preface by Liu Wansu. The original was probably called Yifang jingyao xuanming lun 醫方精要宣明論, the title mentioned in Liu's 1186 introduction to his Sunen bingji qiyi baoming ji (see below). Referred to as Xuanming lun in Ma Zongsu's introduction to Liu's Neijing yunqi yaozhi lun (next entry).

—. Neijing yunqi yaozhi lun 内經運氣要旨論 [Treatise on the Essential Purport of Yunqi in the Inner Canon] (#00188). Originally in 9 pian and 3 juan, with an undated preface by Liu. Date unknown, but before Liu's Yuanbing shi, whose preface mentions

## PDF第658页

this book by its title. The received edition (collated and slightly augmented by Ma Zongsu 馬宗素; Fan Xingzhun [1986: 137] dates it to 1195) is titled Xinkan tujie Suwen yaozhi lun 新刊圖解素問要旨論 [Newly-Carved Treatise on the Essential Purport of the Basic Questions, with Diagrammatic Explanations], in 8 juan. Survives only in a Qing hand copy preserved at Guotu. A modern edition based thereon appears in LiuWYQ: 191-270.

——. San xiao lun 三消論 (#06524), in 1 juan. In juan 13 of Zhang Congzheng's Rumen shiqin 儒門事親 (q.v.) under the title "Liu Hejian xiansheng sanxiao lun" 劉河間先生三消論. Written after the Yuanbing shi, which it mentions. Colophon dated 1244 (jiachen 甲辰 year) by editor. Allegedly obtained by Ma Zhengjun 麻徵君 from Liu's descendants before the fall of the Jin in 1234. The colophon mentions three unpublished works in the hands of Liu's descendants: the Sanxiao lun 三消論 itself, the Qiyi 氣宜 (Suwen bingji qiyi baoming ji?) and the Bingji 病機 (Suwen xuanji yuanbing shi?).

——. Shanghai zhige 傷寒直格 (#00822), in 3 juan; date unknown. Edited by Ge Yong 葛壅 of the Yuan dynasty. First extant ed. is from 1328.

——. Suwen bingji qiyi baoming ji 素問病機氣宜保命集 (#00185), in 3 juan; author's preface 1186, first printed in 1251 (preface by Yang Wei 楊威 from that date); earliest extant edition from Liu Hejian shanghan sanshu 劉河間傷寒三書 (1431). Usually referred to as Baoming ji. Yang Wei claimed he obtained a manuscript of it from a former Jin imperial physician who lived in Shandong shortly after the fall of the Jin. I cite from Liu WYQ, which is based on Wu Mianxue's 吴勉學 Gujin yitong zhengmai quanshu 古今醫統正脈全書 ed. (1601).

——. Suwen xuanji yuanbing shi 素問玄機原病式 [“Method for tracing illnesses to their origin with the mysterious mechanism of the Basic Questions”] (#00184), in 1 juan; before 1186 (because it is probably one of the three books discussing the “mysterious mechanism” that Liu mentioned in his 1186 preface to Suwen bingji qiyi baoming ji [首述玄機刊行於世者已有宣明等三書]). Usually referred to as Yuanbing shi. I cite from the SKQS ed. and from Liu WYQ, which is based on a 1431 ed., the earliest extant edition. The book was titled Suwen yuanjing shi 素問元機原病式 in the SKQS edition because of a taboo on the personal name of the Kangxi emperor, Xuanye 玄燁.

——. Xuanming lun 宣明論. See Liu's Huangdi Suwen xuanming lun fang.

——. Yaozhi lun 要旨論. See Liu's Neijing yunqi yaozhi lun.

——. Yuanbing shi 原病式. See Liu's Suwen xuanji yuanbing shi above.

(compiled by later people). Liu Hejian shanghan sanshu 劉河間傷寒三書 (#11562). 1431. Contains the earliest extant editions of Liu's Yuanbing shi, Xuanming lun, and Baoming ji.

——(compiled by later people). Liu Hejian yixue liushu 劉河間醫學六書 (#11563).

1601. Contains three more books than Liu Hejian shanghan sanshu (see previous entry)—Shanghan zhige lunfang 傷寒直格論方 (q.v.), Shanghai biaoben xinfa leicui 傷寒標本心法類

## PDF第659页

萃 (#00823; both attributed to Liu), and Liu Hejian shanghan yijing 劉河間傷寒醫經 (#00825; compiled by Ma Zongsu 馬宗素)— and two appended works—Liu Hong's 錙洪 Shanghai xinyao 傷寒心要 and Zhang Zibe shanghan xinjing bieji 張子和心鏡別集 (by Zhang Congzheng 張從正 [q.v.], edited by Chang De 常德).

Liu Wenshu 劉溫疏. Yunqi aozhi 運氣奧旨. Lost?

LiuWYQ. Liu Wansu yixue quanshu 劉完素醫學全書. Editor in chief Song Naiguang 宋乃光. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2006.

Long Huitang 龍繪堂. Chunzi yi 蠢子醫 [“Medicine for sots” or “The sot physician”] (#11027), in 4 juan plus an appendix; prefaces from 1882 (author's), 1908, and 1910. In ZYJ-4: 926-1018. Designed to be transmitted to the author's grandchildren. The modern editors say that this book displays the strengths of the itinerant physician without suffering from its crudeness (有鈴醫之特長 而無鈴醫之庸俗).

Lou Ying 樓英 (1332-1400; zì Quanshan 全善; bao Quanzhai 全齋). Neijing yunqi leizhu 內經運氣類注 (#00327), in 1 juan; 1388. Has survived as juan 40 of the 1565 edition of Lou's Yixue gangmu (see next entry). Lou's original preface calls the work Yunqi buzhu 運氣補注 (“Supplementary notes on the five periods and six Qi”).

Yixue gangmu 醫學綱目 [Systematic Outline of Medical Learning] (#11511), in 40 Juan. Final version completed in 1396. First printed in 1565 (edited by Cao Zhuo 曹灼 [bao Lǔzhai 履齋], assisted by Shao Bian 邵弁 [zì Weiyuan 偉元], and Liu Huaqing 劉化卿 [his zì]; 1565 preface by Cao; short colophon by Shao). "Ming-Qing mingzhu congkan" 明清名著叢刊 ed. (Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1996), collated and annotated by A Jing 阿靜, Yan Zhian 闫志安, and Niu Jiuwang 牛久旺, based on the 1565 edition at CATCM. An "opening fascicle" (首卷) added by the 1565 editors contained the original preface of Lou's Neijing yunqi leizhu (q.v.), as well as a treatise compiled by the 1565 editors on illness prognostication by means of wuyun liuqi precepts. That text was prefaced by Shao Bian, the one of the three editors who owned either the original manuscript of Lou's book or an early copy thereof (as discussed in Cao Zhuo's 曹灼 preface). Cao, the main editor, was an official in the Ministry of Punishments in the late Jiajing reign.

Lu He 盧和. See Zhu Zhenheng, Danxi xiansheng yishu zuanyao 丹溪先生醫書纂要.

——. Shiwu bencao 食物本草 (#02743), in 4 juan. First extant ed. from 1570.

Lüliguan jiaozheng Xiyuanlu 律例館校正洗冤錄, in 4 juan; 1674. Compiled by the Office of Codification (律例館).

Luxin jing 顱顱經 [Fontanelle Classic] (#07247), in 2 juan; 10 $ ^{th} $ century. Author unknown.

Earliest extant edition from SKQS.

Lu Yitian 陸以湉 (jinshi 1836, d. 1865; zì Dingpu 定圃; from Tongxiang 桐鄉 in Zhejiang).

Lenglu yihua 冷盧醫話 (#10742), in 5 juan. Author's preface 1858. Editions from 1858, 1897, and late Qing. ZYC ed. (1999) based on the 1897 edition.

## PDF第660页

Luo Denggao 駱登高. Yilin yizhi 醫林一致 [Unity in the Forest of Medicine] (#05006), in 5 juan; several prefaces from the spring of 1703, announcing the first printing. At CATCM.

Luo Mei 羅美 (zì Dansheng 澹生, bao Dongyi 東逸). Gujin mingyi fanglun 古今名醫方論 (#03097), in 4 juan; author's preface 1675. Original Guhuai tang 古懷堂 ed. in Zhongguo kexueguan library, Zhongguo yixue kexueyuan library, and Beida Library.

——. Gujin mingyi huicui 古今名醫匯萃 (#11522), in 8 juan; 1675. Manuscript from 1675 at Harbin yike 醫科 daxue library; printed version in Guhuai tang 古懷堂 ed. of Gujin mingyi buicui Gujin mingyi fanglun bekan 合刊 at Guangxi Zhongyi xueyuan library. ZYC ed. in simplified characters by Yi Guangqian 伊广谦 and Zhang Huifang 张慧芳, based on an 1823 Shengxin fu 盛新甫 (Jiaxing 嘉興) edition.

Luo Tianyi 羅天益 (zī Qianfu 謙甫). Weisheng baojian 衛生寶鑒 [Precious Mirror on Safeguarding Life] (#04884), in 24 juan with a supplement (buyi 補遺); 1281. Earliest surviving ed. is in Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃 (1308 or 1315; q.v.). Re-edited and printed in Suzhou in 1417 by a man surnamed Han 韓 (copy at the CATCM).

___. Dongyuan shixiao fang 東垣試效方. See under Li Gao.

LuoYQ. Luo Tianyi yixue quanshu 羅天益醫學全書. Editor in chief 許敬生. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2006.

Ma Wenzhi 馬文植 (zī Peizhi 培之; 1820-1903). Ma Peizhi yian 馬培之醫案. Late 19 $ ^{th} $ or early 20 $ ^{th} $ century. First printed in 1923 in SSYS on the basis of a manuscript in the collection of SSYS's editor Qiu Qingyuan 裘慶元. In SSYS-1: 827-48.

Maishu 脈書 [Vessel Book]; ca. 200 BCE. In Zhangjiashan Hanmu zhujian (er si qi bao mu) 張家山漢墓竹簡 (二四七號墓). Beijing: Wenwu chubanshe, 2001.

Ming Code. See Dai Ming lũ.

Mingshi明史. See History of the Ming.

Ming shilu. See Dai Ming shilu.

Ming Wuzong shilu 明武宗實錄. “Veritable Records” of the Zhengde emperor (r. 1506-1521) of the Ming dynasty. See Dai Ming Shilu.

Mingyi leian. See under Jiang Guan 江瓘.

Miscellaneous Writings of Enlightened Physicians. See Wang Lun, Mingyi zazhu 明醫雜著.

Mo Meishi 莫枚士. Yanjing yan 研經言 (#10739), in 4 juan; author's preface from 1856. Earliest print from 1879, at CATCM.

MQXC. "Ming-Qing Zhongyi linzheng xiao congshu" 明清中醫臨證小叢書. A collection in simplified characters at the Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe.

## PDF第661页

MYYH. Zhongguo lidai mingyi yibua daguan 中國歷代名醫醫話大觀. 2 vols. Main editors Shen Hongrui 沈洪瑞 and Liang Xiuqing 梁秀清. Taiyuan: Shanxi kexue jishu chubanshe, 1996. In simplified characters.

Nakagami Kinkei 中神琴溪 (1744-1835). Seisei tō chicken 生生堂治驗 (#10098), in 2 juan.

Nanjing. See Canon of Problems.

New Book of Tang. Xin Tangshu 新唐書. Compiled by Ouyang Xiu 歐陽修 (1007-72) and Song Qi 宋祁 (998-1061). Presented in 1060. Zhonghua shuju ed.

Old Book of Tang. Jiu Tangshu 舊唐書. Compiled by Liu Xu 劉煦 (887-946). Presented in 945. Zhonghua shuju ed.

Pan Ji 潘楫 (1592-1666). Yideng xuyan 醫燈續焰 (#01414), in 21 juan; 1652. Based on Cui Jiayan's 崔嘉彥 (zī Zixu 子虛) Siyan maijue 四言脈訣, re-edited under the title Siyan juyao 四言舉要 by Li Yanwen 李言聞 (zī Ziyu 子鬱) during the Ming, and supplemented and annotated by Pan Ji on the basis of the teachings of his master Wang Shaolong 王紹隆 (1566-1624). Copies of 1652 ed. in many libraries, including CATCM. One undated ed. also survived.

Pang Anshi 龐安時 (1043-1110). Shanghai zong bing lun 傷寒總病論 [General Treatise on Cold Injury Illnesses] (#00812), in 6 juan, 1100. SKQS ed.

Pei Yizhong 裴一中 (from Haining 海寧 in Hangzhou prefecture and then from Haiyan 海鹽 in Jiaxing 嘉興 prefecture in Zhejiang). Peizi yanyi 裴子言醫 (#10652), in 4 juan (preface from 1657). In MYYH-1: 165-214.

Prescriptions Worth a Thousand in Gold. See Sun Simiao, Qianjin fang 千金方.

Probing the Mysteries of the Golden Casket. See Zhu Zhenheng, Jingui gouxuan 金匱鉤玄.

Puji fang 普濟方 [Recipes for Universal Succor] (#02995), in 426 juan; completed in 1406. Edited by Zhu Su 朱肅.

Pu Songling 蒲松龄. Liaozhai zhiyi 聊齋誌異.

Qizheng hui 奇症匯 (#10092), in 8 juan, 1786. Compiled by Shen Yuan 沈源. First and only known ed. at CATCM.

Qianjin fang. See under Sun Simiao.

Qianlong chao shangyu dang 乾隆朝上諭檔. 18 vols. Edited by the First Historical Archives of China 中國第一歷史檔案館. Beijing: Dang'an chubanshe, Beijing Xinhua shudian suo faxing, 1991.

Qianlong shilu 乾隆實錄. Dai Qing Gaozong Chun buangdi shilu 大清高宗純黃帝實錄. “Veritable Records” of the Qianlong emperor (r. 1736-1796). Volumes 17-46 of Dai Qing shilu (q.v.).

## PDF第662页

Qian Yi 錢乙 (1026/38 – 1107/19;⁴ zi Zhongyang 仲陽; from Yunzhou 鄲州/Dongping 東平, Shandong, then a center of medical studies). Xiaoer yaozheng zhijue 小兒藥證直訣 (#07248; see also #07249, #07284, and #07561), in 3 juan; 1119; bou xu by Qian from 1093. Collated by Qian's admirer Yan Jizhong 閻季忠. The Siku quanshu reconstructed the book from the Yongle dadian according to its original structure, which was explained in Yan's preface.

QianYQ. Qian Yi, Liu Fang yixue quanshu 錢乙劉昉醫學全書. Editor in chief Li Zhiyong 李志庸. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2005.

Qing Code. Refers to the codified law of the Qing dynasty, which appeared under various titles between its first edition in 1647 and its last updated version in 1870. The titles included Dai Qing liü 大清律 (1647), Dai Qing liü jijie fuli 大清律集解附例 (1725), and Dai Qing liüli 大清律例 (after 1740).

Qingdai wenziyu dang 清代文字獄檔 [Archival Documents on Qing-Dynasty Literary Trials]. 1986 [1931]. Edited by Gugong bowuyuan wenxian guan 故宮博物院文獻館. 2 vols. Qingdai lishi ziliao congkan 清代歷史資料叢刊. Shanghai: Shanghai shudian, Xinhua shudian Shanghai faxing. Original from Beiping: Gugong bowuyuan wenxian guan, 1931.

Qingshi gao 清史稿 [Draft History of the Qing], in 529 juan; sompleted in 1927. Edited by Zhao Erxun 趙爾巽 et al. Zhonghua shuju edition.

Qingyuan tiaofa shilei zuan 庆元条法事类纂 [Itemized laws of the Qingyuan period (1095-1100)]. Collated by Xie Shenfu 謝深甫 (1139-1204) and others in the early thirteenth century. Re-edited in 80 juan by Beijing: Zhongguo shudian, 1990.

Qiu Qingyuan 裘慶元. See SSYS, ZYD.

Qiushen jiyao 秋審輯要, in 6 juan; 1884. Edited by Gang Yi 岡毅. Original edition kept at National Taiwan University Library. There is also an 1889 version in 8 juan published by Jiangsu shuju 江蘇書局.

Qiushen tiaokuan 秋審條款 [Autumn Assizes Regulations]. First published in 1762.

Quan Tang shi 全唐詩, in 900 juan. Beijing: Zhonghua shuju, 1999.

QWD. See Qingdai wenziyu dang.

RBXC. Riben xiancun Zhongguo xigou guyixue congshu 日本現存中國稀觀古醫學叢書, ed. by Ma Jixing 馬繼興 et al. Beijing: Renmin weisheng chubanshe, 1999. Photo-reproductions of rare Chinese medical texts preserved in Japanese libraries.

Recipes of the Imperial Medical Service. See Taiyiju fang 太醫局方.

## PDF第663页

Recipes of the Pharmacy Service. See Hejiju fang 和劑局方.

Recipes of Sagely Compassion. See Taiping shenghui fang 太平聖惠方.

Recipes for Universal Relief. See Shenyi pujiu fang 神醫普救方.

Renzong Rui huangdi shilu 仁宗睿皇帝實錄. “Veritable Records” of the Jiaqing emperor (r. 1796-1820). Volumes 47-53 of Dai Qing shilu (q.v.).

RMSQ. See Zhang Congzheng, Rumen shiqin 儒門事親.

Ruan Xiaoxu 阮孝緒 (479-536). Wenzi jilue 文字集略, in 1 juan.

Sansan yishu 三三醫書 (#11862), 1923. Compiled and edited by Qiu Qingyuan 裘慶元 (see SSYS). Three-volume edition in simplified characters at Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1998.

Sanyin fang. See Chen Yan, Sanyin jiyi bingyuan luncui 三因極一病源論粹.

Sand Seal. See Li Zhongzhi, Bingji shazhuan 病機沙篆.

Seng Shen yaofang 僧深藥方. Liu-Song dynasty (420/79). Lost.

The Scholars, in 56 chapters. Rulin waishi 儒林外史, by Wu Jingzi 吴敬梓 (1701-1754).

Shanghan lun. See Treatise on Cold Injury.

Shanghan zabing lun. See Treatise on Cold Injury and Miscellaneous Disorders.

Shao Da. See Huangfu Zhong, Mingyi zhizhang.

Shatumusu 沙圖穆蘇 (zī Qianzhai 謙齋). Ruizhu tang jingyan fang 瑞竹堂經驗方 [Recipes Proven Effective from the Hall of Auspicious Bamboo] (#02985), originally in 15 juan; 1323. SKQS ed. (5 juan and 188 recipes) reconstructed from extracts in the Yongle dadian. There is also a 1795 Japanese ed. based on a Ming ed. (15 juan and 315 recipes; at CATCM). The 1878 Danggui caotang 當歸草堂 ed. (which also appears in Ding Bing's 丁丙 Danggui caotang yixue congshu 醫學叢書, 1878) is based on the limited SKQS. Shatumusu is the Romanization of the Chinese transliteration of a Mongolian name.

Shen Jiaben 沈家本 (1840-1913). Xuxue tang gushi shansheng 敘雪堂故事刪剩. In Han Yanlong et al. 2006 (see Bibl. B).

Shen Jin'ao 沈金鰲 (1717-1776; zì Qianlù 芊綠; bao Jimen 汲門; late-year bao 尊生老人; from Wuxi 無錫 in Jiangsu). Zabing yuanliu xizhu 雜病源流犀燭 (#05050), in 30 juan; author's preface from 1773; first printing completed in late 1774 or early 1775. Two independent editions from 1784 and 1874 have survived, as well as several versions from Shenshi zunsheng shu 沈氏尊生書 (#11656), including some from 1773 and 1784 (the latter at CATCM). I cite from ShenJYQ, which is based on the first edition.

Shen Juren 沈菊仁 (zī Laixiang 來享, studio name Jiufeng huancui shanfang 九峰環翠山房; b. 1851/61; from Yuanhe 元和 [in modern Suzhou]). Shen Juren yian 沈菊仁醫案 (#10179), in 2 juan; preface from 1875. Edited by his disciples. In ZGZC-17.

ShenJYQ. Shen Jin'ao yixue quanshu 沈金鰲醫學全書. Editor in chief Tian Sisheng 田思胜. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Shen Kuo 沈括. Liang fang 良方 [Excellent Recipes]; late 11 $ ^{th} $ century. Lost, but see Su-Shen liangfang 蘇沈良方.

___. Lingyuan fang 靈苑方 (1048/77). Lost.

——. See also Su-Sben liangfang 蘇沈良方.

Shennong bencao jing 神農本草經 [The Divine Husband's Canon of Materia Medica] (#02230). I cite from the Shennong bencao jing jizhu 輯注 edition (Beijing: Renmin weisheng chubanshe, 1995), editor in chief Ma Jixing 馬繼興, with annotations on the differences between all known editions. The text covers pp. 1-461 and is followed by more than 700 pages on the philological research that contributed to the establishment of this edition.

Shenyi pujiu fang 神醫普救方 [Divine physician's recipes for universal relief], in 1000 juan. Completed in 986. Lost.

Shen Yuan 沈源. See Qizheng bui 奇症匯.

Shen Zhiqi 沈之奇. Dai Qing lü jizhu 大清律輯註 [Collected Annotations on the Code of the Great Qing]. 1715. Two-volume ed. at Beijing: Falü chubanshe, 2000, collated and punctuated by Huai Xiaofeng 懷效鋒 and Li Jun 李俊.

Shen Zhongwei 沈仲緯. Xingtong fu shu 刑統賦疏, in 1 juan; prefaces from 1339 and 1341. Late-Qing ed. found in Zhenbilou congshu 枕碧樓叢書. A commentary on Fu Lin's 傳霖 Song-era Prose Poem on the Song Code (Xingtong fu 刑統賦).

Shen Zufu 沈祖復 (zì Li'an 禮庵, bao Fengjiang 奉江; 1862-1925; from Wuxi 無錫 in Jiangsu). Yiyan suibi 醫驗隨筆 (#11061), in 1 juan; 1923. In SSYS-3: 119-40.

Shenghui fang. See Taiping shenghui fang 太平聖惠方.

Shengji zonglu 聖濟總錄 [Comprehensive Records from [the Hall of] Sagely Succor] (#02946). 1110/18. Printed for the first time in 1171 by the Jin with Song blocks. Re-edited by Renmin weisheng chubanshe (Beijing) in 1962.

Shilu. See Dai Ming shilu and Dai Qing shilu.

Shuowen jiezi說文解字; completed in 100 CE. Compiled by Xu Shen 許慎.

Siku quanshu 四庫全書 [Complete Library of the Four Treasuries]. See SKQS.

Sima Guang 司馬光 (1019-86). Leipian 類篇, in 45 juan. 1066. [Taipei]: Taiwan Shangwu yinshu guan, 1983.

Simple and Essential Recipes. See Zhou Ying, Jianyao jizhong fang.

SKCM. Siku quanshu cunmu congshu 四庫全書存目叢書. All the books that were included in the zongmu but not in the final SKQS collection, to which this is an essential supplement.

## PDF第665页

Medical section (yijia lei 醫家類) in zibu 子部. Photo-reproductions of various editions held in Chinese and Japanese libraries, rarely the first editions.

SKQS. Siku quanshu 四庫全書. Started to be compiled in 1771; completed in 1782. Contains 3461 different works.

Song Ci 宋慈 (1186-1249). Xiyuan jilu 冼冤集錄, in 5 juan.

Song Code. See Song Xingtong.

Song Lian 宋濂 (1310-81). "Epitaph." See Song's "Gu Danxi xiansheng Zhu gong shibiao ci."

——. “Gu Danxi xiansheng Zhu gong shibiao ci” 故丹溪先生朱公石表辞 [Eulogistic epitaph of Danxi xiansheng, Sire Zhu]. Probably 1358. In Song Wenxian gong quanji 宋文憲公全集 [Sibu beiyao 四部備要 ed.], juan 50. Also appended to the end of juan 5 of the Huizhou edition of the Danxi xinfa 丹溪心法 (see under Zhu Zhenheng). I cite from Danxi yijii (2000): 323-28.

——. Song Wenxian gong quanji 宋文憲公全集, in 75 juan. The original, which was called Song xueshi quanji 宋學士全集, was printed for the first time between 1506 and 1521. Sibu beiyao 四部備要 edition based on a Jiaqing (1796-1820) edition.

___. Wenxian ji 文憲集, in 32 juan. SKQS ed.

___. "Ti Zhu Yanxiu yi mo hou" 題朱彥修遺墨後, 1370s. In Song's Wenxian ji 文憲集, juan 13.

Song xingtong 宋刑統, in 30 juan; 963. Compiled by a team led by Dou Yi 竇儀.

Punctuated edition established by Wu Yiru 吴翊如 at Beijing: Zhonghua shuju, 1984.

SSYS. San san yi shu 三三醫書, 1923. Compiled and edited by Qiu Qingyuan 裘慶元. Three-volume edition in simplified characters at Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1998.

Subtle Meanings. See Liu Chun and Xu Yanchun, Yuji weiyi 玉機微義.

Su-Shen liang fang 蘇沈良方 [Excellent Recipes by Su (Shi) and Shen (Kuo)] (#02940), in 10 juan; 12 $ ^{th} $ century. A collection of recipes from the writings of Su Shi 蘇軾 (1036-1101) and Shen Kuo 沈括 (1031-1095). Original preface by Shen Kuo 沈括. Draws mainly from Shen’s Excellent Recipes (q.v.). SKQS ed. based on the Yongle dadian 永樂大典.

Su Shi. See Su-Shen liangfang.

Sun Cailin 孫采鄰, Zhuting yian 竹亭醫案 (#10310), in 9 juan; 1911. In ZGZC-9, based on the original manuscript found at the Shanghai Library.

Sun shangyao fang 孫尚藥方 [Recipes by Sun of the Palace Medical Service]. Usually attributed to Sun Yonghe 孫用和, but could have been compiled by his son Sun Zhao 孫兆, who also served in the Palace Medical Service.

## PDF第666页

Sun Simiao 孫思邈. Qianjin fang 千金方 [Prescriptions worth a thousand in gold] (#02932), in 30 juan (the SKQS ed. is in 93 juan); 652/59. Also known as Beiji qianjin yaofang 備急千金要方 and Qianjin yaofang 千金要方. I cite the SunSYQ and the SKQS eds.

SunSYQ. Sun Simiao yixue quanshu 孫思邈醫學全書. Editors in chief Han Xuejie 韓学杰 and Zhang Yinsheng 张印生. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2009.

Sun Wenyin 孫文胤. Dantai yu'an 丹臺玉案 [Jade Files from the Cinnabar Platform] (#04952), in 6 juan; 1637 preface. Original ed. by Sunshi Renshou Hall 孫氏仁壽堂 [author's edition?] at CATCM. Despite its title, this book was not a collection of medical case records.

Sun Yikui 孫一奎 (1522-1619; zì Wenheng 文恆; bao Dongsu 東宿, Shengshengzi 生生子). Chishui xuanzhu 赤水玄珠 [Pearls of Wisdom from the Crimson Sea], in 30 juan; [1584]. First printed in Chishui xuanzhu quanji (see next entry). The date 1584 is difficult to believe, since the list of works Sun claimed to have consulted in compiling this book included Gong Tingxian's Wanbing buichun 萬病回春 (1587) and Li Shizhen's Bencao gangmu 本草綱目 (which was first printed in Nanjing in 1596, though it had been completed in 1578). I cite from the SKQS ed. and Sun YQ, which is based on a 1596 edition.

Chishui xuanzhu quanji 赤水玄珠全集 (#11582), in 37 juan, first ed. printed in She county by Huang Ding 黄鼎 in [1584?]. Contained Chishui xuanzhu and Yizhi xuyu (see Sun Wenheng yian 孫文恆醫案). The second ed. (1597? 1599?) also contained a collection of Sun's case records divided geographically into three sections. Sanwu yian (2 juan), Xindu yian (2 juan), and Yixing yian. Surviving copies of the first edition at the Shandong provincial library, Jiangxi provincial library, and Shanghai library.

——. Sanwu zhiyan 三吳治驗 (#10023). See Sun Wenbeng yian.

. Sun Wenheng yian 孫文恆醫案 (#10022) (also called Shengshengzi yian 生生子醫案 [from the author's bao], Chishui xuanzhu yian 赤水玄珠醫案 [from the compilation in which the cases were published in the very late 1590s], and Sunshi yian 孫氏醫案), in 5 juan, ca. 1599. Compiled by Sun's disciples Yu Huang 余煌 and Xu Jingqi 徐景奇, as well as his sons Sun Tailai 泰來 and Sun Penglai 朋來. Divided into three sections: juan 1 and 2 called "San Wu zhiyan" 三吳治驗 (154 cases), juan 3 and 4 called "Xindu zhiyan" 新都治驗 (203 cases), and juan 5, titled "Yixing zhiyan" 宜興治驗 (41 cases), divided according to places where Sun practiced medicine. First independent edition: 1599.

___. Xindu zhiyan 新都治驗 (#10024). See Sun Wenbeng yian.

___. Yixing zhiyan 宜興治驗. See Sun Wenbeng yian.

Yizhi xuyu 醫旨緒餘 [Residual (Discussions) on the Purport of Medicine] (#00294), in 2 juan. 1584?. SKQS ed. First printed in Sun's Chishui xuanzhu quanji (q.v.). Earliest extant autonomous edition from 1590. In SunYQ (1999): 639-704.

Sun Yonghe 孫用和. Chuanjia mibao fang 傳家秘寶方. Lost.

___. Chuanjia mibao maizheng koujue bing fang 傳家秘寶脈證口訣並方 (#02943). Partial manuscript copies have survived.

—. See also Sun shangyao fang.

SunYQ. Sun Yikui yixue quanshu 孫一奎醫學全書. Editors in chief Han Xuejie 韓学杰 and Zhang Yinsheng 张印生. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Sun Zhao 孫兆. Shanghai maijue 傷寒脈訣 [Songs on the pulses of Cold-injury]. Late 11 $ ^{th} $ century. Lost?

—. See also Sun shangyao fang.

Suwen. See Basic Questions.

Systematic Outline. See Lou Ying, Yixue gangmu 醫學綱目.

Taki Mototane 多紀元胤 (also known as Tamba no Mototane 丹波元胤; 1789-1827).

Iseki kō 醫籍考 (#12057), in 80 juan. First completed in 1819, and then corrected and enhanced by Mototane's brother Taki Motokata 多紀元堅 (1795-1857), who published the work in 1831. Preface from that year by Taki Motokata. Often cited as Zhongguo yiji kao 中國醫籍考 from the title adopted by the Renmin weisheng chubanshe (Beijing) for their 1983 edition. I cite from the 2007 ed. by the Xueyuan chubanshe (Beijing), arranged by Guo Xiumei 郭秀梅 and Okada Kenkichi 岡田研吉, based on Fujikawa Yū's 富士川遊 photo-reproduction of the handwritten original (Fujikawa's edition was published at the Kokuhon shuppansha 國本出版社 [Tokyo] in 1935; the author's name in that edition was indicated as Taki Mototane).

Taiping guangji 太平廣記, in 500 juan, completed in 978. Compiled by a team led by Li Fang 李昉.

Taiping huimin hejiju fang. See Hejiju fang 和劑局方.

Taiping shenghui fang 太平聖惠方 [Taiping-era Recipes of Sagely Compassion] (#02938), in 100 juan; compiled from 978 to 992 by Wang Huaiyin 王懷隱 and others; first published in 992.

Taiping yulan 太平預覽, in 1000 juan; 984. Compiled on imperial order by a team led by Li Fang 李昉.

Taisu. See Grand Basis.

Taiyiju fang 太醫局方 [Recipes of the Imperial Medical Service], in 3 juan; 1078/85 (Yuanfeng 元豐 era) of Song Shenzong. A corrected and supplemented edition of this book became the Hejiju fang (q.v.) in the the Daguan 大觀 era (1107-10) of Song Huizong. Lost.

Taiyiju zhuke chengwen ge 太醫局諸科程文格 (#10628), in 9 juan. 1212. SKQS ed.

Tamba no Mototane. See Taki Mototane.

## PDF第668页

Tang Code. Tanglü shuyi 唐律疏議, in 30 juan. Compiled by a team led by Zhangsun Wuji 長孫無忌 (d. 659). Presented to the throne in 653. Slightly revised edition presented in 737. Beijing: Zhonghua shuju, 1996. Edition established and punctuated by Liu Junwen 劉俊文.

Tanglũ shuyi. See Tang Code.

Tao Hua 陶華 (zī Shangwen 尚文, bao Jie'an 節庵). Shanghai liushu 傷寒六書 [Six Books on Cold Injury] (#00837), in 6 juan; 1445. Composed of six works, each in 1 juan: Shanghan jiami diben 傷寒家秘的本, Shanghai mingli xulun 傷寒明理續論, Shanghai suoyan 傷寒瑣言, Shanghai shache chuifa 傷寒殺車槌法, Shanghai yi ti jin qimeng 傷寒一提金啟蒙, Shanghai zhengmai jiejiang wang 傷寒證脈截江閏.

Tiansheng gaijiu xinding lüling 天盛改舊新定律令. Between 1149 and 1169. Modern Chinese translation of the Code of the Western Xia (西夏) dynasty (1038-1227), annotated and translated by Shi Jinbo 史金波, Nie Hongyin 聶鴻音 and Bai Bin 白濱. Zhonghua chuanshi fadian 中華傳世法典. Beijing: Falü chubanshe, 1999.

Tracing the Symptoms to their Origins. See Zhubing yuanhou lun.

Treatise on Cold Injury. Shanghai lun 傷寒雜 (#00576), in 10 juan. By Zhang Ji 張機 (Zhongjing 中景, bao Changsha 長沙), 196/220. For more details, see Treatise on Cold Injury and Miscellaneous Illnesses.

Treatise on Cold Injury and Miscellaneous Illnesses. Shanghai zabing lun 傷寒雜病論. By Zhang Ji 張機 (zì Zhongjing 中景, bao Changsha 長沙), 196/220. Quickly lost, this work was reconstituted in the late third century by Wang Xi 王熙 (zì Shuhe 叔和), re-edited sometime between 968 and 975, and again in 1064 or 1065, when it was split into three books: the Treatise on Cold Injury (q.v.), the Essentials of the Golden Casket (q.v.), and the Canon of the Jade Envelope in the Golden Casket (Jingui yuhan jing 金匱玉函經).

Treatise on Symptoms. See Zhubing yuanbou lun.

UCDA. Union Catalogue of Digital Archives, Taiwan [Shuwei diancang lianhe mulu 數位典藏聯合目錄]. http://catalog.digitalarchives.tw/dacs5/System/Catalog/Catalog.jsp. Includes archives of the Grand Council (http://catalog.digitalarchives.tw/dacs5/System/Catalog/List.jsp?CID=6706) and archives of the Grand Secretariat (http://catalog.digitalarchives.tw/dacs5/System/Catalog/List.jsp?CID=824).

Ui-bang yuch-wi [Yifang leiju] 醫方類據 (#04776), in 266 juan; 1445. Compiled by a team led by Kim Ye-mong 金禮蒙.

Waitai biyao. See Wang Tao.

Wang Tao.

Wang Ang 汪昂 (1615-1699; zǐ Ren'an 認庵; from Huizhou in Anhui). Bencao beiyao 本草備要 [Essentials of Materia Medica] (#02303), in 4 juan; 1694.

Bencao yifang hebian 本草醫方合編 (#11622). Joint edition of Wang's Bencao beiyao and Yifang jijie (see these two entries). Surviving eds.: Kangzi (3), 1740 (2), 1777,

## PDF第669页

1778, 1787, Qianlong (4), 1841, 1844 (2), 1845, 1851, 1855, 1868, 1883, 1884, 1887, 1888 (2), 1891, 1894, 1895, 1896, 1898, 1900, 1904, 1908 (2), Guangxu, 1909, Qing (28)

——. Tangtou gejue 湯頭歌訣 [Rhymed Songs on Decoctions] (#03341), in 1 juan; 1694.

——. Yifang jijie 醫方集解 [Collected Explanations on Medical Formulas] (#03100), in 3 juan; 1682.

Wang Ao 王鏊 (1450-1525; zì Jizhi 濟之; a 5B official when he wrote his preface). Gu danfang 古單方 (#03010); in 4 juan; author's preface from 1496. Before it was published, the book was augmented by the author's younger brother Wang Quan 王銓 (zì Bingzhi 秉之; was Registrar in Hangzhou Prefecture 杭州府經歷), edited and reorganized by Physician (醫士) Xu Zhongfu 忠甫 (his zì), and printed at the author's behest by Secretariat Drafter 中書舍人 (ranked 7B) Wang Yanzhe 王延喆 at an unknown date between 1496 and 1525. The book was reprinted in 1548 and renamed Bencao danfang 本草單方 [copy at the Shanghai library]. 1999 ZYC ed. by Liu Zhengzhe 刘正哲 based on a Jiaqing-era (1796-1820) hand copy (at CATCM) of Wang Yanzhe's edition.

Wang Bing. See Basic Questions.

WangBYQ. Wang Bing yixue quanshu 王冰醫學全書. Editors in chief Zhang Dengben 张登本 and Sun Lijun 孙理军. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2006.

Wang Gui 王珪 (1264-1354?; zǐ Junzhang 均章, Yiren 逸人; bao Dongxuzi 洞虚子, Zhongyang laoren 中陽老人; a.k.a. Wang Yinjun 隱君). Taiding yangsheng zhulun 泰定養生主論 [#09590], in 16 juan, 1338. Earliest complete edition is 1511 Maoluan 冒鸞 ed., held at the National Library, photoreproduced in SKCM-41: 187-304.

Wang Gun 王衰. Boji fang 博濟方 [Recipes for Broad Succor] (#02939), in 5 juan; 1041/48 (the Qingli 慶曆 reign period of Song Renzong). The earliest edition is the SKQS recreation of the original.

Wang Haogu 王好古 (13 $ ^{th} $ century; zì Jinzhi 進之, bao Haicang 海藏 and Haicang laoren 海藏老人). Cishi nanzhi 此事難知 (#00830), in 2 juan; preface dated 1308 claiming to be from the author.

——. Tangye bencao 湯液本草 [“Materia medica for Decoctions and Brews” or “Materia medica for [Yi Yin’s 伊尹] Decoctions and Brews”] (#02246), in 3 juan. Three prefaces by the author from 1238, 1246, and 1248. The 1238 preface mentions Wang’s Yilei yuanrong, Yinzheng lieli (qq.vn.), Banlun cuiying 癡論萃英 (a.k.a. Haicang 海藏 banlun cuiying) and Qianshi buyi 錢氏補遺 (lost). Tangye bencao is the only extant work by Wang Haogu that does not appear in Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃 (q.v.). There is a 1335 edition. The title refers to the Canonical Methods of Decoctions and Brews (Tangye jingfa 湯液經法), attributed to the ancient sage Yi Yin, which Wang considered to be at the root of Zhang Ji’s prescription in the Treatise on Cold Injury (q.v.). WangHYQ ed. based on Wu Mianxue’s 吳勉學 Gujin yitong zhengmai quanshu 古今醫統正脈全書 ed. (1601).

## PDF第670页

Yilei yuanrong 醫壘元戎 [Medical Ramparts against the Yuan Barbarians] (#04881), in 12 juan. Completed in 1231, but left unprinted; stolen in the spring of 1237 and purportedly rewritten from memory on that same year; preface from the 9 $ ^{th} $ month of 1237 (from last paragraph of the book [WangHYQ: 334]). SKQS ed. Also in Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃, Dongyuan shishu 東垣十書 (qq.vn.), and Wu Mianxue's 吳勉學 Gujin yitong zhengmai daquan 古今醫統正脈全書 (1601).

——. Yinzheng lieli 陰證略例 [Elementary Examples of Yin Symptoms] (#00828), in 1 juan. Probably completed in 1236 (final note); author's preface from 1232. Other preface from 1243, the date of an announced publication. WangHYQ ed., based on a 1879 ed. by Lu Xinyuan from Guian 歸安陸心源, taken from the Shiwan juan lou congshu 十萬卷樓叢書 (original at CATCM). Eleventh work in Jisheng bacui 濟生拔萃 (q.v.).

WangHYQ. Wang Haogu yixue quanshu 王好古醫學全書. Editor in chief Zhang Dengben 張登本. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2004.

Wang Ji 汪機 (1463-1540;⁴ zì Shengzhi 省之, hao Shishan 石山 [Stone-mountain]; from Qimen county 祁門縣 in Huizhou prefecture). Shishan yi'an 石山醫案 [Stone-Mountain's Medical Case Files] (#10019), in 3 juan. Preface by Cheng Zeng 程曾 dated 1531. Edited by Wang's disciple Chen Jue 陳桷. I cite from Wang JYQ (1999: 57-115), which is based on a 1633 ed. by Wang Wei 王惟 (at Shanghai Library), and from the SKQS ed., which omits some sub-titles.

___. Bencao huibian 本草匯編. Mentioned in the 1523 bibliography of Wang Ji appended to the Shishan yian.

——. Douzhi libian 痘治理辨 (#07690), in 1 juan. Compiler's preface from 1531, printed in or soon before 1534.

——. Du Suwen chao 讀素問抄 [Studying the Suwen chao] (#00154), in 3 juan, each further separated into 4 sections. Compiler's preface from 1519; printed in 1522. Based on Hua Shou's 滑壽 Suwen chao.

——. Majue kanwu 脈訣刊誤, in two juan plus one appendix; compiler's preface from 1523 announcing printing and claiming that the book had been completed years earlier. This is Wang Ji's edition of a Yuan work in 2. juan called Majue kanwu, by Dai Tongfu 戴同父; the appendix is by Wang Ji himself. Called Chongji Majue kanwu 集集脈訣刊誤 [“A Re-Compilation of Majue kanwu”] in the bibliography of Wang Ji (dated 1523) that is appended to Shishan yian.

——. Suwen buzhu 素問補注. Mentioned in the 1523 bibliography of Wang Ji appended to Shishan yian. May have referred to Wang's Du Sunven chao (see above).

——. Tuiqiu shiyi 推求師意. 1534. See under Dai Sigong 戴思恭.

## PDF第671页

——. Waike lili 外科理例 [External medicine: doctrine and examples] (#08377), in 7 juan, plus one appended juan of prescriptions. Author's preface from 1531; printed in or before 1533.

——. Yixue yuanli 醫學原理, in 13 juan; undated compiler's preface. Apparently unpublished until around 1600, when Wu Mianxue 吴勉學 edited and printed it.

——. Yunqi yilan 運氣易覽 [The Easy-to-Peruse (Five) Periods and (Six) Qi], in 3 juan and 40 sections. Compiler's preface from 1528; printed for the first time by Wang's disciple Cheng Qiao 程鑄 (colophon from 1533). A short intertextual treatise on an influential cosmological scheme. Was also published as juan 5 of Xu Chunfu's 徐春甫 Gujin yitong daquan 古今醫統大全 (1556) (q.v.). The WangJYQ ed. is based on the 1533 edition.

——. Zhenjiu wendui 針灸問對 [“Questions and answers on acumoxa”], in 3 juan and 85 questions-and-answers. Compiler's preface from 1530; printed in 1532.

Wang Jiufeng. See Wang Zhizheng.

Wang Jihuan. See Zhu Zhenheng, [Danxi xinfa 丹溪心法].

WangJYQ. Wang Ji yixue quanshu 汪機醫學全書. Editor in chief Gao Erxin 高尔鑫. Ming-Qing mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 1999.

Wang Kentang 王肯堂 (1552-1638). Chongding Linglan yaolan 重訂靈蘭要覽 (#04932), in 2 juan; 1602? I cite from MYYH-1: 93-124, which is based on the SSYS ed. (1923). First printed with added notes and commentaries by Gu Jinshou 顧金壽 (xi Xiaolan 曉瀾) in the Daoguang years (1821-1850). Also in ZYD.

——. Zhengzhi zhunsheng 證治準繩 (#11589), in 44 juan. 1602. Original edition at the Zhongguo kexueyuan library.

Wang Leting 王樂亭. Wang Leting zhiyao 王樂亭指要 (#08468); 1875. In ZGZC-5.

Wang Lü 王履 (zì Andao 安道; from Kunshan 崑山). Yijing suhui ji 醫經溯洄集 [#10631], in 2 juan; 1368. "Early Ming" ed. at Shanghai Library and Nanjing Library. Also in Dongyuan shishu 東垣十書 (1529), Gujin yitong zhengmai quanshu 古今醫統正脈大全 (1601), and SKQS.

Wang Lun 王綸 (1453-1510;  $ \ddot{z} $i Ruyan 汝言, bao Jiezhai 節齋). Bencao jiyao 本草集要 [Collected Essentials of Materia Medica] (\#02252), in 8 juan; 1492. First extant ed. from 1510.

——. Mingyi zazhu 明醫雜著 [Miscellaneous Writings of Enlightened Physicians] (#04903), in 6 juan; 1502. Hongzhi [1488-1505] ed. at Zhejiang Provincial Library. I cite from the version edited by Xue Ji 薛己 (1487-1559; with editor's preface dated 1551) that was later published in Xueshi yian 薛氏醫按 [Notes on Medicine by Mr. Xue], and from the SKQS ed. of Xueshi yian (see that work under Xue Ji).

Wang Shixiong 王士雄 (1808-1868; zi Mengying 孟英). Guishuo lu 歸碩錄 (#11002), in 4 juan; 1859. First edition at CATCM.

## PDF第672页

——. Gujin yian an xuan 古今醫案按選 (#10144), in 4 juan; 1853. A selection from Yu Zhen's 俞震 Gujin yian an (q.v.). First printed in 1904 (at CATCM). Also in ZYJ-4: 185253.

——. Wangshi yian 王氏醫案 (#10134), in 10 juan; 1850. Contains Wangshi yian zhengbian 正編 (in 2 juan) and Wangshi yian xubian 續編 (in 8 juan). 1851 ed. at CATCM.

___. Wenre jingwei 温熱經緯 (#05707), in 5 juan. Two prefaces, including author's, from 1852. Edition from that year at CATCM. Surviving editions from 1852, 1863, 1864, 1874, Tongzhi, 1877, 1882, 1885, 1892 (2), 1893, 1895, 1896, 1897, 1903, 1904, 1905, Guangxu (2), Qing (2+).

—. See also Xu Dachun's Huixi yian.

Wang Shuhe. See Canon of the Pulse.

Wang Tailin 王泰林 (1798-1862; zì Xugao 旭高). $ ^{5} $ Liangxi Wangshi waike yian 梁溪王氏外科醫案; edited by Chu Baoren 褚寶仁. Manuscript.

. Pingxuan huanxi caotang yian 評選環溪草堂醫案 (#10224), in 3 juan; edited by Liu Baoyi 柳寶詒 and published in 1900 (with annotations) in Liu xuan si jia yian 柳選四家醫案 (MQXC ed., 1997).

——. Wang Xugao linzheng yi'an 王旭高臨證醫案 (#10222), 1897. Annotated edition by Fang Renyuan 方仁淵 (zi Gengxia 耕霞), with some modifications. In ZYJ-4: 273-366.

——. Wang Xugaoyian buyi 王旭高醫案補遺; edited by Xu Xiangting 徐湘亭. Manuscript.

——. Huanxi caotang yian 環溪草堂醫案. Edited by Chu Xuanren 褚玄仁 in 1996 on the basis of the above four titles, removing Fang Renyuan's and Liu Baoyi's emendations but preserving their commentaries. Designed to be exhaustive. In Wang Xugao yishu quanji 王旭高醫書全集 (Beijing: Xueyuan chubanshe, 2001): 263-454. Chu Xuanren claims that this edition is based on a manuscript that Wang Xugao collated late in his life.

Xixi shuwu yehua lu 西溪書屋夜話錄 [Records of evening talks at the Westbrook studio]. Published for the first time in the 1920s. In Chu Xuanren (ed.), Wang Xugao yishu quanji 王旭高醫書全集 (Beijing: Xueyuan chubanshe, 2001): 69-81.

——. Yixue chuyan 醫學芻言 [My Humble Views on Medical Learning]. First published as Yimen yaojue 醫門要訣 (#05160). In Chu Xuanren (ed.), Wang Xugao yishu quanji 王旭高醫書全集 (Beijing: Xueyuan chubanshe, 2001): 7-33.

Wang Tao 王燾 (ca. 670-755). Waitai biyao 外臺秘要 [Arcane Essentials from the Outer Terrace (i.e., the Imperial Library)] (#02936), in 40 juan; author's preface 752. The edition closest to the original is the Song edition reproduced in Tōyō igaku zempon sōsho 東洋醫學全本叢書, vol. 3. There is also a complete Song edition at the National

## PDF第673页

Library in Beijing. The most common edition (by Cheng Yandao 程衍道, 1640), on which all later important editions are based, modified the Song edition in some respects (see Okanishi 1958: 678). I cite from WangTYQ, which is based on the Tōyō igaku zempon sōsho reproduction and on three other editions.

WangTYQ. Wang Tao yixue quanshu 王濤醫學全書. Editor in chief Zhang Dengben 張登本. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2006.

Wang Xi 王熙. See Canon of the Pulse.

Wang Xugao 王旭高. See Wang Tailin.

Wang Yixiang 汪藝香 (1838-1900; from She county in Huizhou). Wang Yixiang xiansheng yian 汪藝香先生醫案. Copied and edited by many hands between 1924 and 1934. In ZGZC-19.

Wang Yingzhen 王應震 (dates unknown). Wang Yingzhen yaojue 王應震要訣 [“Wang Yingzhen’s essential rhymes”] (#10208). ZGZC-15 ed., based on Guangxu-period (1875-1908) manuscript held at the Shanghai Library, which is the only surviving copy.

Wang Zhizheng 王之政 (zi Xianting 獻廷, bao Jiufeng 九峰; 1735-1830?; from Dantu 丹徒 in Jiangsu). Wang Jiufeng yian 王九峰醫案 (#10107), in 3 juan and two appendices. In ZGZC-13, based on hand copy kept at Shanghai Zhongyiyao daxue library. That copy was made in the Republican era by Zhu Fangyi 朱方依 on the basis of cases by Wang copied by Xu Yinting 徐蔭庭 and Zhu's disciple Jiang Baosu 蔣寶素. Also in ZYC, based on a copy in 2 juan previously held by Zou Heyu 鄒鶴瑜 from Wuxi. The ZYC ed. contains more madness cases.

Wang Zhizhong 王執中. Zhenjiu zisheng jing 鍼灸資生經 (#01748), in 7 juan, first printed in 1220. SKQS ed. There is also a Yuan edition at Guotu.

Wei Yilin 危亦林 (1277-1347). Shiyi dexiao fang 世醫得效方 [Successful Recipes from Physicians of this World] (#02988), in 20 juan; preface from 1337, printed in 1345. I cite from SKQS and from Wei YQ, which is based on 1345 ed.

WeiYQ. Wei Yilin yixue quanshu 危亦林醫學全書. Editor in chief Xu Jingsheng 許敬生. Tang-Song-Jin-Yuan mingyi quanshu dacheng. Beijing: Zhongguo Zhongyiyao chubanshe, 2004.

Wei Zhi 衛直 (zì Guiyan 桂岩; bao Tingbao 廷豹). Bo'ai xinjian 博愛心鑒 (#07692), in 2 juan; 1525. Ming edition available at the Shanghai Zhongyi xueyuan.

Wenchang. See Huasbu.

Wen Cunhou 溫存厚 (zī Zaizhi 載之; from Yuzhou 渝州). Wenshi yian 溫氏醫案 (#10195), in 1 juan; 1886. Ed. from that year at Beijing Zhongyi xueyuan Library. Also 1887 ed. Also in Wenshi yishu sanzhong (see entries below) and attached to Wen's Wenbing qianshuo (see entry below).

___. Wenbing qianshuo 温病淺說 (#05731). Eds. from 1886 (at CATCM) and 1887. Also in Wenshi yishu sanzhong (see below).
